debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
A : Set U
f : ↑A → V
v : V
⊢ func_restrict (func_extend f v) A = f

proof:
by
  funext x
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  <;>
  aesop
failed_attempts:
by
  funext x
  -- Since x is in A, we can use the property of the restriction and extension of functions
  have hx : (x : U) ∈ A := by exact x.property
  -- Simplify the expression using the properties of the functions and the hypothesis hx
  simp [hx, func_restrict, Subtype.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

by
  apply eq_of_heq
  apply Eq.refl

by
  have h_main : ∀ (a : A), func_restrict (func_extend f v) A a = f a := by sorry
  sorry

by
  funext
  have h₀ : (A : Set U) = Set.Icc (A : Set U) (A : Set U) := by sorry
  have h₁ : value_of_mem_of_mem (by sorry : @A : Set U) (by sorry : value) = value := by sorry
  have h₂ : value ∈ A → f value = f value := by sorry
  sorry

by
  apply funext
  intro x
  simp [func_restrict, Set.mem_coe, iff_def]
  <;> by_cases hx : x ∈ A <;> simp_all [hx]
  <;>
  (try contradiction) <;>
  (try simp_all [Set.mem_coe]) <;>
  (try aesop)
h₁

by
  have h : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ A → (func_restrict (func_extend f v) A) ⟨x, by assumption⟩ = f ⟨x, by assumption⟩ := by
    intro x hx
    simp [func_restrict, Membership.mem_setOf_eq, hx, func_extend]
    <;> aesop
  ext ⟨x, hx⟩
  have h₁ : (func_restrict (func_extend f v) A) ⟨x, hx⟩ = f ⟨x, hx⟩ := h x hx
  simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

by
  funext x
  have h₁ : x ∈ A := x.prop
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, h₁]
  <;> aesop

by
  ext a
  simp [Function.funext_iff]
  <;> aesop

by
  ext x <;> simp_all [Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Subtype.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

by
  funext ⟨x, hx⟩
  simp [hx]
  <;> aesop

by
  have h_main : ∀ (x : A), (func_restrict (func_extend f v) A) x = f x := by
    intro x
    have h₁ : (x : U) ∈ A := x.prop
    simp [h₁, func_restrict, func_extend]
    <;> simp_all
    <;> aesop
  
  funext x
  apply h_main

by
  ext a
  simp [func_restrict_apply, func_extend_apply, Subtype.mk.injEq]
  <;> aesop

by
  ext x
  simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq, ForallForall]
  <;> aesop
by sorry

by
  funext x
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend_apply]
  <;> aesop

by
    apply funext
    intro x
    by_cases hx : x ∈ A
    · -- Case: x ∈ A
      simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Function.funext_iff, func_restrict, func_extend]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Case: x ∉ A
      simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Function.funext_iff, func_restrict, func_extend]
      <;> aesop

by
  ext x hx
  simp [hx, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  <;> aesop

by
  funext ⟨x, hx⟩
  simp [hx]
  <;> aesop

by
  funext a
  -- For any `a : A`, we need to show that `func_restrict (func_extend f v) A a = f a`.
  -- Since `a : A`, the `else` branch of `func_extend f v` will never be taken when restricting to `A`.
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend]
  -- Simplifying the expressions, we see that the `else` branch cannot occur because `a ∈ A`.
  <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  -- The simplifier will reduce the expression to `f a`, as required.
  <;> aesop

by
  funext x
  simp [Subtype.ext_iff, Function.funext_iff]
  <;> aesop

a.property
      simp [h]
      <;> aesop
    )

by
  ext x
  simp [subtype.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop
successful_attempts:
by
  funext x
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  <;>
  aesop

by
  funext x
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, Subtype.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

by
  funext x
  -- For any x : A, we need to show that (func_restrict (func_extend f v) A) x = f x.
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  -- Simplifying the left-hand side using the definitions of func_restrict and func_extend.
  <;>
  aesop
  -- Using aesop to handle the logical conditions and conclude the proof.

by
  ext ⟨x, hx⟩
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, hx]
  <;> aesop

by
  funext x
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend, x.prop]
  <;> rfl

by
  funext a
  simp [func_restrict, func_extend]
  <;> aesop
